scope_note: Only publicly visible sources were used. This file does not claim to cover every basketball shooting product in the world.

sources.name: HomeCourt
sources.relevance: Public product page states that the app tracks makes, misses, attempts, shooting percentage, progress over time, and skill ratings.

sources.name: Noah Basketball
sources.relevance: Public product page lists shot arc, depth, left-right data, and gives benchmark values of 45 degrees arc, 11 inches depth, and 0 inches left-right.

sources.name: ShotTracker
sources.relevance: Public product page states it provides real-time stats, lineup efficiencies, video breakdowns, and more than 70 advanced statistics.

sources.name: MediaPipe Pose Landmarker
sources.relevance: Official Google AI Edge documentation describes pose landmark detection for images and video, which is a credible basis for a future pose adapter.

sources.name: Penner, 2021
sources.relevance: The referenced research summary reports that the shot dip shows a U-shaped relationship with three-point shooting percentage in NBA tracking data.

sources.name: Barzykina et al., 2025
sources.relevance: The paper's abstract describes prediction of shot success from player position and orientation variables, supporting ArcAI's event-and-body-orientation API fields.

sources.name: Zhu et al., 2026
sources.relevance: The paper describes using temporal graph neural networks to predict ground reaction forces and moments from marker data, relevant to future GRF estimator design.

sources.name: Stripe Checkout
sources.relevance: Official Stripe documentation covers creating a hosted checkout session; ArcAI's prototype keeps payments behind a server endpoint until keys and products are configured.
